file_name: BABCOCK_WILCOXENTERPRISES,INC_08_04_2015-EX-10.17-INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY AGREEMENT between THE BABCOCK _ WILCOX COMPANY and BABCOCK _ WILCOX ENTERPRISES, INC..txt

perturbation.original_text: RemainCo and SpinCo acknowledge and agree that the Shared Library Materials represent a collection of shared historical and foundational information related to and derived from more than fifty (50) years of research and development focused on technology and design criteria applicable to the businesses of RemainCo and SpinCo, which information, including all Know-How and Copyrights contained therein, has application to the businesses of RemainCo and SpinCo.
perturbation.changed_text: RemainCo and SpinCo acknowledge and agree that the Shared Library Materials represent a collection of shared historical and foundational information related to and derived from more than fifty (50) years of research and development focused on technology and design criteria applicable to the businesses of RemainCo and SpinCo, which information, including all Know-How and Copyrights contained therein, has application to the businesses of RemainCo ONLY.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the end of Section 4.1, where the original text states that 'Shared Library Materials' have applications to both RemainCo and SpinCo's business, to only RemainCo's, it creates an in-text contradiction with Section 4.2 which states the materials have relevance to both parties businesses. This creates an uncertainty of enforcement, as it is unclear whether the licensing grants of 4.2 are valid if the use of the Shared Library Materials is only stated to be useful for only one of the parties in 4.1.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Parties acknowledge and agree that each of RemainCo and SpinCo and additional members of the RemainCo Group and SpinCo Group presently have shared access to and rights to exploit the Shared Library Materials and may have a right and interest in and to certain Shared Library Materials, including in and to Intellectual Property embodied therein. Accordingly, (i) with respect to RemainCo's right and interest in and to the Shared Library Materials, RemainCo, for itself and as representative of all other members of the RemainCo Group, hereby grants to SpinCo (x) a perpetual (subject to Section 4.4), irrevocable, exclusive, royalty-free, worldwide right and license with the right to grant sublicenses (solely as set forth in Section 5.6) to use the Shared Library Materials, including all Know-How and Copyrights embodied therein, for any purpose in the SpinCo Core Field and (y) a perpetual (subject to Section 4.4), irrevocable, non-exclusive, royalty-free, worldwide right and license with the right to grant sublicenses (solely as set forth in Section 5.6) to use the Shared Library Materials, including all Know-How and Copyrights embodied therein, for any purpose in any field other than the RemainCo Core Field or the SpinCo Core Field and (ii) with respect to SpinCo's right and interest in and to the Shared Library Materials, SpinCo, for itself and as representative of all other members of the SpinCo Group, hereby grants to RemainCo (x) a perpetual (subject to Section 4.4), irrevocable, exclusive, royalty-free, worldwide right and license with the right to grant sublicenses (solely as set forth in Section 5.6) to use the Shared Library Materials, including all Know-How and Copyrights embodied therein, for any purpose in the RemainCo Core Field and (y) a perpetual (subject to Section 4.4), irrevocable, non- exclusive, royalty-free, worldwide right and license with the right to grant sublicenses (solely as set forth in Section 5.6) to use the Shared Library Materials, including all Know-How and Copyrights embodied therein, for any purpose in any field other than the SpinCo Core Field or the RemainCo Core Field. The foregoing licenses include the right to reproduce, prepare derivative works, distribute, perform and otherwise exploit such Shared Library Materials and to use the Shared Library Materials to design, develop, manufacture, have manufactured, sell and support products and services, subject in each case to applicable export control Laws and the provisions of Section 4.5.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 4.1 now states that 'Shared Library Materials' have applications to only RemainCo's business, while the contradicted text in Section 4.2 states that both RemainCo and SpinCo have shared access to and rights to exploit the Shared Library Materials. This is a direct contradiction, as one section limits the application to RemainCo only, while the other grants rights to both parties, creating uncertainty about the licensing grants in 4.2 if the use of the Shared Library Materials is only useful for one party, adhering to the definition of Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Each Party shall promptly notify the other Party in writing upon learning that a Third Party may potentially be infringing, misappropriating or otherwise violating any Intellectual Property licensed under this Agreement, which notice shall set forth in reasonable detail the identity of the suspected infringer and nature of suspected infringement.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party may choose to notify the other Party in writing upon learning that a Third Party may potentially be infringing, misappropriating or otherwise violating any Intellectual Property licensed under this Agreement, which notice shall set forth in reasonable detail the identity of the suspected infringer and nature of suspected infringement.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'shall' to 'may choose to' in Section 8.2, it contradicts the explicit 'No Obligation' mentioned in the beginning of Article VIII in Section 8.1. The original text creates a contradictory obligation where there is none, while the 'may choose to' inserted text now creates an allowance when Article VIII's intention is that there is 'no obligation' to begin with, creating contradictory allowances and intentions of the obligation to notify of infringement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: No Party shall have any obligation to institute or maintain any action or suit against any Third Party for infringement or misappropriation of any Intellectual Property licensed hereunder, or to defend any action or suit brought by a Third Party which challenges or concerns the validity of any such Intellectual Property or which claims that any Intellectual Property licensed to the other Party infringes or constitutes misappropriation of the Intellectual Property rights of any Third Party.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates notification ('shall notify') upon learning of potential IP infringement, which implies an obligation. The changed text alters 'shall' to 'may choose to,' removing the mandatory obligation. This directly contradicts the 'No Party shall have any obligation' clause in the contradicted text, as the modified clause provides an allowance where the contradicted clause explicitly states there is no obligation. This constitutes a strong in-text contradiction because the altered text creates conflicting intentions regarding the obligation to notify of infringement, which could lead to disputes over contractual obligations and unenforceability of terms relating to IP protection.

perturbation.original_text: The Parties acknowledge that it is desirable for future editions of the Steam Book to present a comprehensive view of how steam is generated and used from a variety of energy sources including, inter alia, nuclear energy sources. SpinCo shall have the sole right to determine the content contained in future editions of the Steam Book, provided, however, that SpinCo will provide RemainCo with a right of first refusal to collaborate with SpinCo to provide content related to nuclear subject matter in the forty-third (43rd) edition of the Steam Book, with allocation of costs and revenues and any attribution and Copyright ownership with respect to RemainCo contributed content to be negotiated in good faith by the Parties.
perturbation.changed_text: The Parties acknowledge that it is desirable for future editions of the Steam Book to present a comprehensive view of how steam is generated and used from a variety of energy sources including, inter alia, nuclear energy sources. SpinCo shall have the sole right to determine the content contained in future editions of the Steam Book, provided, however, that SpinCo will deny RemainCo with a right of first refusal to collaborate with SpinCo to provide content related to nuclear subject matter in the forty-third (43rd) edition of the Steam Book, with allocation of costs and revenues and any attribution and Copyright ownership with respect to RemainCo contributed content to be negotiated in bad faith by the Parties.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'SpinCo will provide RemainCo with a right of first refusal to collaborate with SpinCo to provide content related to nuclear subject matter' to 'SpinCo will deny RemainCo with a right of first refusal to collaborate with SpinCo to provide content related to nuclear subject matter', there is an immediate contradiction. The first sentence states that it is desirable to have nuclear energy information included in future additions of the Steam Book, yet the modified text immediately after, actively denies the party with nuclear-specific information to collaborate on providing nuclear subject matter content, directly contradicting the desirability.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Parties acknowledge that it is desirable for future editions of the Steam Book to present a comprehensive view of how steam is generated and used from a variety of energy sources including, inter alia, nuclear energy sources. SpinCo shall have the sole right to determine the content contained in future editions of the Steam Book, provided, however, that SpinCo will deny RemainCo with a right of first refusal to collaborate with SpinCo to provide content related to nuclear subject matter in the forty-third (43rd) edition of the Steam Book, with allocation of costs and revenues and any attribution and Copyright ownership with respect to RemainCo contributed content to be negotiated in bad faith by the Parties. The foregoing right of first refusal shall cease in the event of a Change of Control of either RemainCo or SpinCo, provided, however, that the Parties will discuss the feasibility of future collaboration, specifically related to the contribution of nuclear related subject matter by RemainCo, in good faith in the event of a Change of Control.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original intent and the repeated acknowledgement of the desirability of including nuclear energy information in future editions. The original text states that it is desirable to have nuclear energy information included in future additions of the Steam Book, yet the modified text immediately after, actively denies the party with nuclear-specific information to collaborate on providing nuclear subject matter content. The contradicted text is essentially the same text with the contradiction.
